summary: Completed testing of ASP.NET Core 8 backend migration from Python/FastAPI. All 17 requested features verified working. Fixed one critical issue (ambiguous route match for /api/pricing/active). Backend 16/16 tests pass, frontend 3/3 login flows work.

fixed.issue: Ambiguous route match - conflicted with /api/pricing/{pricingId} and duplicate minimal API in Program.cs
fixed.fix: Removed duplicate minimal API endpoint from Program.cs

authentication: POST /api/auth/login with admin@rxexpresss.com / admin123 returns access_token and user object
POST /api/auth/login with pharmacy@test.com / pharmacy123 returns access_token
POST /api/auth/login with driver@test.com / driver123 returns access_token
GET /api/auth/me with valid token returns current user
admin_dashboard: GET /api/admin/dashboard returns stats object with total_users, total_orders, total_pharmacies, total_drivers, active_drivers, orders_by_status
GET /api/admin/orders returns orders list with total count
pricing: GET /api/pricing/active returns active pricing list (no auth required)
api_structure: JSON responses use snake_case as configured in Program.cs
frontend_integration: Admin login and view dashboard works correctly
Driver login and view portal works correctly
Pharmacy login and view dashboard works correctly

critical_code_review_comments: .NET backend successfully migrated from Python FastAPI with all controllers working
JWT authentication working with issuer='RxExpresss' and audience='RxExpresss'
JSON serialization correctly configured with snake_case naming policy
MongoDB integration preserved - using same database and collections
seed_data_creation: Used existing test users from MongoDB (shared with Python backend)
context_for_next_testing_agent: ASP.NET Core 8 backend migration complete and verified. The .NET backend is running on port 8001 and serves all API endpoints. Python backend supervisor has been stopped. JWT tokens use issuer/audience='RxExpresss' - users need to re-login after migration. All MongoDB data preserved. Mocked API: Stripe checkout creates mock session ID instead of real Stripe session.

admin_login.details: Returns access_token and user object with role=admin

admin_dashboard.details: Returns stats with total_users=13, total_pharmacies=2, etc.

pricing_active.details: Returns active pricing (no auth required) - Fixed ambiguous route issue

frontend_admin_login.details: Admin can login and view dashboard with stats

frontend_driver_login.details: Driver can login and view portal with deliveries

frontend_pharmacy_login.details: Pharmacy can login and view orders

mocked_apis.stripe_checkout: Returns mock session ID instead of real Stripe session

fix_applied.root_cause: Duplicate endpoint definitions - minimal API in Program.cs (lines 99-110) and controller method GetActivePricing(). The {pricingId} route parameter also matched 'active' as a string.
fix_applied.solution: Removed duplicate minimal API endpoint from Program.cs. Controller's [HttpGet("active")] now correctly handles the route.
